agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-window commitment, premium fee, and refund policy. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a specialized fulfillment mode within the broader Order/Delivery bounded context, with its own eligibility rules, SLA tracking, driver assignment constraints, and refund policies.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option with a stated 2-hour delivery window and higher fee, subject to eligibility constraints (distance, driver rating)."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"The $12.99 surcharge charged for selecting Express Delivery, separate from the base delivery fee."},{"term":"Base Delivery Fee","definition":"The standard delivery charge applied to all orders, not refunded under the express SLA miss policy."},{"term":"Delivery Window","definition":"The 2-hour period from order placement within which the express delivery should be completed."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The 15-minute window from order placement within which a driver must pick up the express order from the warehouse."},{"term":"Warehouse Proximity","definition":"The 10km maximum distance between customer and warehouse required for express eligibility."},{"term":"Driver Rating Threshold","definition":"Minimum 4-star driver rating required for assignment to express deliveries."},{"term":"Replacement Window","definition":"The 10-minute window to assign a replacement driver after a mid-delivery cancellation before an automatic refund is triggered."},{"term":"Wrong Address Exception","definition":"A refund exclusion applied when the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, voiding SLA miss compensation."},{"term":"Goodwill Refund","definition":"Legal's characterization of the express fee refund — a discretionary gesture, not a contractual obligation."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Express delivery option is only available to customers located within 10km of a warehouse.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — beyond 10km the 2-hour window is operationally infeasible. Offering it would create systemic SLA failures.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer address >10km from all warehouses, WHEN they view delivery options, THEN express delivery is not offered."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Express orders must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational policy to protect the 2-hour window. The threshold could be tuned based on operational data.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express order placed at T, WHEN no driver has picked it up by T+15min, THEN the system flags it as a pickup SLA breach."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Only drivers with a rating of 4 stars or above may be assigned to express deliveries.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational quality policy — the threshold could be adjusted as driver pool or rating system evolves.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver with a 3.9-star rating, WHEN the system considers them for an express order, THEN they are excluded from assignment."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing is a business policy decision subject to change by Finance/PM.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer selects express delivery, WHEN the order is confirmed, THEN the total includes a $12.99 express surcharge on top of the base delivery fee."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"When the delivery window is missed, only the express fee is refunded — not the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund scope is a business policy. Could change based on competitive pressure or customer feedback.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery completed after the 2-hour window, WHEN a refund is processed, THEN only $12.99 is refunded, not the base delivery fee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"No refund is issued when the customer provided an incorrect address, even if the delivery window is missed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business policy — wrong-address exception is a fraud/fairness control, adjustable by policy.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery missed its window AND the order is flagged with an incorrect customer address, WHEN refund eligibility is evaluated, THEN the refund is denied."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes or an automatic refund of the express fee is triggered.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Customer Success policy to limit customer impact from driver cancellations. Threshold and auto-refund behavior are tunable.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver cancels an in-progress express delivery at time T, WHEN no replacement driver is assigned by T+10min, THEN the express fee is automatically refunded."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Express fee refunds are processed within 3 business days.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Finance operational SLA — subject to change based on payment infrastructure or policy.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express fee refund is approved, WHEN 3 business days elapse, THEN the refund has been processed to the customer's payment method."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"Customers can track their express delivery driver in real time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Product feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer has an active express delivery, WHEN they view the order status, THEN they see the driver's real-time location on a map."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer is within 10km of a warehouse","when":"They view delivery options","then":"Express delivery is shown as available with the 2-hour window and $12.99 fee displayed prominently"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer is more than 10km from all warehouses","when":"They view delivery options","then":"Express delivery option is hidden or shown as unavailable with an explanation"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An express order is placed","when":"15 minutes elapse without driver pickup","then":"The system escalates the order (alerting ops) and flags it as a pickup SLA breach"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An express delivery is completed after the 2-hour window AND the address was correct","when":"The system evaluates refund eligibility","then":"A $12.99 express fee refund is initiated, processed within 3 business days"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An express delivery misses its window AND the customer gave a wrong address","when":"The system evaluates refund eligibility","then":"No refund is issued; the customer is informed of the wrong-address exception"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A driver cancels an in-progress express delivery","when":"10 minutes elapse without a replacement driver being assigned","then":"An automatic express fee refund is triggered without requiring manual approval"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A driver cancels an in-progress express delivery","when":"A replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes","then":"The delivery continues, the 2-hour window remains measured from original order placement, no refund is triggered"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An express delivery is in progress","when":"The customer views their order","then":"Real-time driver location is displayed on a map with estimated arrival time"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"The system is assigning a driver for an express order","when":"Eligible drivers are filtered","then":"Only drivers with a rating >= 4.0 stars are considered for assignment"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Driver picks up at minute 14, delivery arrives at 2h01m — barely missed both SLAs","expected_behavior":"Pickup SLA was technically met (under 15 min). Delivery window was missed. Refund eligibility should be evaluated normally (check for wrong address exception). The near-miss pickup should not affect refund."},{"scenario":"Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse","expected_behavior":"Boundary condition — must decide if 10km is inclusive or exclusive. Recommendation: inclusive (<=10km is eligible). Must be consistent across all eligibility checks."},{"scenario":"Driver cancels at 1h50m into delivery — replacement window (10 min) would exceed the 2-hour delivery window","expected_behavior":"Both the replacement window (10 min) and delivery window (2h) are tracked. If no replacement in 10 min, auto-refund. If replacement assigned but delivery exceeds 2h, standard SLA-miss refund applies. Both refund triggers should not stack — only one express fee refund."},{"scenario":"All eligible drivers (4+ stars) in range are busy","expected_behavior":"System cannot assign an express order. Need a policy: queue and wait (risking pickup SLA), downgrade to standard delivery with notification, or reject express option proactively. THIS IS A GAP — no stakeholder addressed it."},{"scenario":"Customer changes address after order placement to one >10km from warehouse","expected_behavior":"Policy gap — does the express guarantee still apply? Should the system re-validate eligibility? Could this constitute a 'wrong address' scenario?"},{"scenario":"Multiple SLA miss triggers on the same order (e.g., driver cancels AND replacement also exceeds 2h window)","expected_behavior":"Only one express fee refund per order. Refund triggers should be idempotent — the $12.99 is refunded once regardless of how many triggers fire."},{"scenario":"Customer places express order at 11:55 PM — 2-hour window crosses midnight","expected_behavior":"Window is clock-agnostic (2 hours from placement). But operational capacity may be reduced at night. No stakeholder addressed hours of availability for express."},{"scenario":"Pickup SLA breached (>15 min) but delivery still completes within 2 hours","expected_behavior":"No customer-facing consequence — the 2-hour window is the customer-facing commitment. Pickup SLA breach is an internal ops metric only."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Express delivery at $12.99 creates a premium revenue stream. Refund exposure is bounded to the express fee per order. Key risk: if SLA miss rates are high, refund volume could erode margins. The auto-refund on driver cancellation (BR-7) creates an uncapped automatic liability — Finance needs visibility into cancellation rates.","operational":"15-minute pickup SLA creates significant dispatch pressure. The 4-star driver requirement reduces the eligible driver pool, potentially creating a supply constraint that causes more SLA misses. Real-time tracking adds infrastructure cost (GPS streaming, map rendering). The 10-minute replacement window requires a robust re-assignment engine.","user":"Customers get a premium fast-delivery option with real-time visibility. The prominent 2-hour display sets strong expectations. However, the legal/PM contradiction on 'guarantee' vs 'best effort' creates a CX risk — customers will perceive this as a guarantee regardless of legal fine print.","risk":"The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is the highest risk item. If marketed as a guarantee but legally disclaimed as best-effort, customer trust erodes on the first missed delivery. Additionally, auto-refunds without human review could be exploited (e.g., coordinating with drivers to cancel). The wrong-address exception needs clear determination criteria to avoid disputes.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Gain a premium fast-delivery option with tracking, but confusion risk if 'guarantee' language is inconsistent across touchpoints."},{"group":"Drivers","impact":"Higher-rated drivers get more express assignments (earnings opportunity). Lower-rated drivers are excluded. Express orders add time pressure."},{"group":"Warehouse Operations","impact":"Must support 15-minute pickup readiness for express orders, requiring prioritization in pick/pack workflows."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle disputes around wrong-address exceptions, near-miss SLA cases, and confusion about guarantee vs. best-effort language."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"New revenue stream but also new refund liability. Need monitoring of SLA miss rates and auto-refund volumes."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Must ensure customer-facing language does not create contractual liability. Need to reconcile PM's 'guaranteed' with Legal's 'best effort'."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"Two direct stakeholder contradictions undermine spec clarity: (1) PM says 'guaranteed' while Legal says 'best effort / not a guarantee', and (2) PM/CS imply refunds are automatic entitlements while Legal frames them as discretionary goodwill. These are not minor — they affect customer-facing language, refund automation logic, and legal exposure. Until resolved, the team cannot safely implement the customer-facing commitment or the refund engine.","high_confidence_areas":["Express fee amount ($12.99)","10km warehouse proximity eligibility rule","15-minute pickup SLA (internal ops)","4-star driver rating minimum","3 business day refund processing window","Wrong-address refund exception","Real-time driver tracking requirement"],"low_confidence_areas":["Express availability hours — no stakeholder addressed whether express is 24/7 or limited hours","What happens when no eligible drivers are available — no policy defined","Whether the 10km boundary is inclusive or exclusive","Address change after order placement — eligibility re-validation policy","How 'wrong address' is determined and by whom — criteria for this exception are undefined","Whether the replacement driver must also meet the 4-star threshold"],"assumptions":["The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not pickup time","Only one express fee refund is issued per order regardless of how many triggers fire","The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (crow flies) from customer to nearest warehouse","Driver rating is evaluated at assignment time, not maintained throughout delivery"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"PM states the 2-hour delivery window is 'guaranteed' and should be shown prominently. Legal explicitly states this CANNOT be guaranteed in writing and must be framed as 'best effort' only. These are mutually exclusive positions that affect all customer-facing language, marketing materials, and the UX copy for the delivery option. This must be resolved before any customer-facing implementation.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Legal","Customer Success","Marketing","Customers"],"options":["Use 'guaranteed' language with a legal disclaimer in fine print (PM wins, Legal adds disclaimer)","Use 'best effort' / 'estimated' language everywhere (Legal wins, PM adjusts expectations)","Use aspirational language like 'aim to deliver within 2 hours' with a refund policy as the backstop (compromise)"],"recommendation":"Option C — use language like 'Express: 2-hour delivery target' with clear refund policy as the customer-facing commitment. This satisfies Legal's constraint while giving PM a strong value proposition. The refund policy does the heavy lifting of building trust."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"PM and Customer Success describe refunds as automatic entitlements (PM: 'we refund the express fee'; CS: 'we refund automatically'). Legal explicitly states the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' This contradiction affects whether the refund engine should be fully automated (as PM/CS expect) or discretionary (as Legal frames it). An automated system that always refunds contradicts Legal's 'goodwill' framing; a discretionary system contradicts PM/CS's 'guaranteed refund' promise.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance","Legal","Customer Success","Product Management","Engineering"],"options":["Fully automated refunds — system auto-refunds on SLA miss, Legal accepts this as standard practice (not legally compelled but operationally committed)","Discretionary refunds — SLA misses trigger a review queue, support decides case-by-case","Automated with override — system auto-refunds by default, but Finance/Support can block in exception cases (fraud, wrong address)"],"recommendation":"Option C — automated refunds with an exception/override mechanism. This satisfies PM/CS expectations for speed and consistency while preserving Legal's position that each refund is technically discretionary. The wrong-address exception (already agreed by Finance) is the first override rule."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-window commitment, premium fee, and refund policy. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes involving PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a specialized fulfillment mode within the broader Order/Delivery bounded context, with its own eligibility rules, SLA tracking, driver assignment constraints, and refund policies.","ub

content.thinking:
Let me analyze these workshop notes carefully for the Express Delivery product.

The key contradiction I see: Product Manager says "guaranteed within 2 hours" while Legal says "We cannot guarantee a 2-hour delivery time in writing — this is a 'best effort' SLA, not a contractual guarantee." This is a direct contradiction between two explicit stakeholder positions.

There's also a tension around the refund: PM says "we refund the express fee" (implying it's automatic/guaranteed), Finance says refunds processed within 3 business days (implying a process), and Legal says "The refund is a goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable." These are related contradictions about the nature of the refund commitment.


Customer Success adds another layer with an automatic refund trigger if replacement isn't assigned within 10 minutes, which directly conflicts with Legal's position that refunds are discretionary goodwill gestures rather than guaranteed obligations. This escalates to a clear contradiction between stakeholders, so I'm marking this as `contradiction` with confidence at 50 or below.
